title: Durable confinement tests
goal: Promote the slice-local grep/confinement guards that protect Pi wire strings and painter event-name boundaries into ordinary Rust tests that run under every cargo test/gate/CI invocation, and record the manual-integration disposition from the superseded blocked run.
acceptance: All existing projection snapshots, parity fixtures, and golden output tests remain behavior-preserving unless an intentional divergence is explicitly documented in this slice output.

Durable Rust confinement tests under tests/confinement.rs scan src/ for the existing Pi wire-string and painter interpretation boundaries that were previously enforced only by slice-local grep verify commands.
The tests make the allowed owners explicit, especially that Pi event wire vocabulary remains owned by the Pi contract layer and renderers/painters do not grow ad-hoc raw event interpretation.
The confinement tests fail with actionable messages that name the violated boundary and the unexpected file/path match.

The manual-integration disposition is documented in a durable project doc or invariant note: commits from the kd-20260707-112420-7f061d72 turbulence were manually integrated because that run was superseded by attention/read-model authority defects and was intentionally not resumed.
The new tests run as part of plain cargo test; no separate slice-specific grep command is required to preserve these boundaries.
must_ask_if: A guard would require forbidding legitimate Pi contract constants or test fixtures rather than checking ownership boundaries.
The implementation needs to weaken, delete, or mark ignored any existing projection/golden/parity test to pass.
The disposition text would claim the blocked KD run completed or was resumed.
The implementation would add a new external linter or CI dependency instead of using ordinary Rust tests.
